# Transcrição: 02-Live Scaling Day 2

- **Idioma:** en

## [00:00 - 03:02]

So this is the second part video looking at the impact of the changes we made on this particular ad set where we went into vertical scaling with a 20% increase on our original ad set and then horizontal scaling by introducing manual bid for those ad sets as well. So this is the data from that initial experiment. So if we have a look at our original ad set here at the top, we've ended the day slightly under our budget, so we increased it 20% midway through the day. The algorithm didn't actually manage to spend it all and that's something which will keep a close eye on in the second day to make sure it is hitting that budget. But positively, the cost acquisition retained the level that it was at before as well, so that's really positive. Now if we look at the other ad sets which had spend, our $30 max bid had the second highest spend, but the cost per acquisition here was a lot higher. So our target is sub $25 and here we've got $44. The duplication we made with no change whatsoever actually started off positively but ended the day with the highest CPA actually almost $60. Our $45 max bid ad set finished off with $22 and a decent amount of budget spent there as well. So this is really positive. So here you can see it's $345. That's because we've increased it 20% today because it's good performance and that's something which when you take an original ad set, you add 20% there, you duplicate it, make a change, if it works again you add 20% there. So here we've got two ad sets that are within range. Our $15 average manual bid. Now the reason to go for $15 is originally when we took this original ad set, it was hovering around $15. So what I wanted to test is if we used $15 as the average bid, could we get conversions in a good CPA? We didn't. Again this is a high CPA ad set and then the final one that we did here which is quite interesting is the max bid $15 and that had zero spend. Now what that tells me is this $15 bid is not high enough to win the option. If you look at the $30 it was high enough to get impressions but not the quality that we were looking for and actually our biting point seems to be $45. So the second part of this test is pausing the $15 and potentially the $30 and actually experimenting around the $45 marks are going for $50, $55 and seeing if we can get even more impressions and a better CPA at that level. Now the other thing to bear in mind is if you look at all of these ad sets together the overall CPA was just actually around our target level. So that's another thing to kind of bear in mind at the blended

## [03:03 - 06:08]

performance as well. Now if we'd taken out some of these highest CPA campaigns our CPA would be even better. Now you know looking at this there's clearly a decision that should be made which is let's pause the ad sets which have not been performing because clearly that's not helping us. However what I want you to also realize is the traffic we're playing with here is at the top of the funnel. This is cold audience and this is where we do all our scaling and testing etc but don't discount the impact of bringing in new traffic on your retargeting. So when you're looking at your performance look at things holistically and break it down from top level down and make sure that you're making the right decision. Now for example our $30 max speed ad set sure the CPA was too high but what I want to do is actually look at how the other metrics perform. So I'm looking at CTR and CPC in particular so click-through rate for our original was 1.2%. Why is it that our $30 max speed is actually performing with a much better click-through rate? So more clicks are coming through on that particular ad set. The cost per click is massively different. You can see from this our original ad set has a lower cost per click. What I probably put that down to is that the ad set is maturer, the ads are maturer as well and so therefore the CPC is good. I would expect that these CPCs would drop down if performance continues as well and then what I'm looking at because we're running videos here is what the watch percent looks like. So our original has just over 12% and again the $30 here is not far off 12% as well so actually it's doing okay. The other ad sets have hit just slightly under. We know from our $45 max speed the CPA was good but again the video view percent was low. So there's a few things that can happen here. If you recall from the previous video we were using the exact same ad sets on the duplicates as we are on the original. One of the things that I actually did was to pause out a few ads on the ad sets here based on the analysis of the first few hours where actually the cost per click and click-through rate on those ads was really bad and Facebook was pushing the traffic into them. So for example taking the $45 max speed ad set we were seeing one particular ad was not performing well. I paused that out and let the other two ads run and we've seen the CPA come in and the thing here is the audience size collectively for this single audience is in the tens of millions. I think it's 40 or 50 million people and I'm dipping these ad sets into the ocean of this big audience and sometimes the audiences will give me a good number sometimes I want but it doesn't mean that the particular ad set has completely failed. There's many different factors and one of them is it could be the ads that are not right for this particular pool of customers that have come through. So when you are

## [06:08 - 09:11]

analyzing your performance do have a look at the ad level and do your ad cycling there as well and see if you can bring in better ads for those ad sets as well. So that's just something to keep in mind especially looking at the metrics where there isn't a massive difference. So then you look at this and say you know this ad set here has had a high spend it's driven let's have a look at the clicks to the site so 129 clicks off the spend of 180 ish dollars so it's actually fairly in line with our winning ad set in fact a better CTR but the number of sales that came through was far lower so it's actually the conversion rate for this particular ad set which has caused it to have a really bad CPA so it's either going to be the cost per click or the conversion rate and for this one it's the conversion rate. So then you kind of look at that and say right can I get the conversion rate up again it goes into ad cycling but what I wanted to do was to see what impact these new ad sets had so considering we were spending 288 dollars on our original we've now scaled that up to close to 2k across all the duplicates what's been the impact on my retargeting so in this window here I've loaded up the retargeting campaigns that I'm currently running for this account I'm looking at the last three days to see how retargeting has actually performed so for this particular campaign we're using website conversion and traffic on exactly the same audience and that's part of the strategy around the kind of hot targeting which we'll be talking about in a separate video but here you can see for example yesterday we had an increase in sales in both of both of our ad sets so with our website conversion we actually had more purchases come through at lower CPA and actually more upsells as well and here we've had again an increase in sales and a drop in our CPA which has given us a better blended performance over that period of time so one decision could be those cold audiences didn't perform let's absolutely cut them and move on but actually I'm looking at this and saying my cost per acquisition for retargeting has dropped and I've got more sales and then I look at the overall performance on the account so here this is the day we're looking at we've managed to increase the actual spend roughly by about 600 dollars overall and based on launching those ad sets partway through the day and we've reduced our CPA and got more purchases and with our CPA target of below 25 dollars overall we've had a good day we've managed to bump up our budget by 600 dollars generate more sales and we've got CPA within target as well and actually you can see today moving on so this this is a client in the US so we're kind of early morning at the moment in the US we've already generated five sales and the CPA has dropped again and that's something which we want to track through the day but the key thing is that you make sure that when you are making

## [09:11 - 11:09]

decisions on your ad sets you're looking at it kind of from top down so I've made these decisions yesterday have increased the budget and actually my overall performance is good and my retargeting therefore because of the the cold traffic that's come through has got more people in there to target as well because what you can't see is what if for example the 30 dollar max bid ad set had actually driven a good number of clicks in and looking at the other stats it looks like it's it's fairly good quality ad set but for some reason they're not converting maybe these guys actually converted in my retargeting campaign as opposed to here where they've whether with a purchase within the actual cold ad set as well so just make sure that when you're analyzing these numbers you're looking across your whole funnel and making the right decision so my decision right now is to leave these running for the time being I want to see how they pan out following another 24 hour cycle and I'm keeping an eye on my overall CPA so if my overall CPA remains good then I'm not going to massively change anything here I'll continue vertically scaling those that are within my cost-prequisition targets but the other ones I'll probably keep until I have confidence of the right decision to make which is to pause or or to continue and and continue the ad cycling within these to try and get the the top level CPA down as well the average order value for this particular website as you can see here is around the 25 dollar mark so it's fairly impulsive it's not a massive commitment so I'd expect a lot of those conversions to come through cold audience because there's not so much to decide upon but that's something to experiment with and make sure that you're making the right decision when you do have this kind of situation where you may look at separate ad sets and not looking so great but overall your performance is good
